definition: Markov-switching GARCH models suffer from a 10-20 day identification lag as a mathematical property of the Bayesian filtering problem, making simple threshold heuristics operationally superior for real-time regime detection. This is the highest-confidence individual claim in the analysis.
evidence: 10-20 day lag is a mathematical property of the filtering problem, not dataset-specific
Documented in Ang & Timmermann (2012) and Hamilton (1989)
Unchallenged in pair_2 debate; assessed as 'well-calibrated' at 8/10
open_questions: Can hybrid approaches combining model output with threshold heuristics outperform either standalone?
What specific threshold heuristics are most effective across different vol regimes?
